items.title: India extends solar cells local sourcing deadline for open access, net metering projects till December
items.summary: India has granted a seven-month exemption to renewable energy projects under open access and net metering, allowing them to use imported solar cells without complying with the ALMM-II (Approved List of Models and Manufacturers for cells) requirement until December 2026. The earlier exemption had lapsed on May 31, after ALMM-II norms kicked in from June 1.

items.title: Govt extends exemption for net metering, open access for renewable energy projects till December
items.summary: The government has extended the exemption for net-metering and open access RE projects. This extension allows these projects to commission without ALMM List-II compliance until December 2026. Previously, this exemption was set to expire on May 31, 2026. The move aims to support domestic solar PV module manufacturers and their existing inventories. This policy supports India's goal of self-reliance in solar manufacturing.

The Mumbai-Ahmedabad bullet train corridor is being constructed at an estimated Rs 1.08 lakh crore.

items.title: MakeMyTrip IPO soon! Online travel giant files confidential DRHP with SEBI, 15 years after Nasdaq listing
items.summary: MakeMyTrip has initiated the process for its India IPO by filing a Draft Red Herring Prospectus with SEBI. The offering involves an equity share sale by MakeMyTrip Limited and its subsidiary, ibibo Group, but details like the issue size remain undisclosed.

items.title: Axis Bank Q1 Results: Net profit jumps 23% YoY to Rs 7,114 crore; NII up 8%
items.summary: Axis Bank Q1 Results: Axis Bank reported a 22.5% YoY rise in Q1 FY27 standalone net profit to Rs 7,114 crore, while net interest income increased over 8% to Rs 14,646 crore, beating estimates. Asset quality remained stable, with the lender reporting strong balance sheet growth, robust deposit mobilisation and healthy loan expansion.

items.title: A new AI order, an oil warning and unrest next door
items.summary: This week Xi Jinping highlighted his country as the champion of a new global AI order, massive protests erupted in Pakistan-Occupied Kashmir and the Hormuz skirmish choked India's oil pipeline again

items.title: The New Oil? Why the world is chasing copper
items.summary: Copper has emerged as a strategic commodity powering the global shift towards clean energy, electric vehicles and digital infrastructure. With demand rising faster than supply due to electrification and constrained mining output, the metal is increasingly viewed as a critical resource for the global economy's long-term transition.

items.title: Indian banks seen poised for strong FY27 on robust credit growth and healthy asset quality
items.summary: Strong loan demand, improving deposits and policy support expected to drive earnings

items.title: El Niño is a warning: It is time to regenerate Indian agriculture is now
items.summary: The country needs a much larger mission, one that restores soils, conserves water, revitalises biodiversity and rebuilds the ecological foundations of agriculture

items.title: Top 5 Large and Midcap Mutual Funds With Best SIP Returns in 5 Years: Rs 30,00,000 one-time investment in No. 1 fund led to Rs 70.85 lakh wealth
items.summary: Top 5 Large & Midcap Mutual Funds With Best Returns in 5 Years: As of July 18, these are the top 5 large and midcap mutual funds with the highest returns over the previous five years. According to calculations, a Rs 30,00,000 one-time investment in the top fund has grown to Rs 70.85 lakh in just 5 years.

items.title: EU rejects Chinese rice flour cargo for presence of GMOs
items.summary: An EU notification issued on Monday said the Netherlands had found the presence of GMOs in hydrolysed rice flour

items.title: Concurrent Gainers: 13 smallcap stocks gain for 5 days in a row
items.summary: Thirteen BSE SmallCap stocks rallied 10-15% after posting gains in each of the last five trading sessions, outperforming the broader market's modest advance.

items.title: Agentic AI, alternative data and SIFs take centre stage at Indian Institutional Quant Conference 2026
items.summary: Agentic AI, alternative data and Specialised Investment Funds (SIFs) dominated discussions at the Indian Institutional Quant Conference 2026, where industry leaders, academics and policymakers explored the evolving role of quantitative investing, AI-driven strategies and regulatory developments shaping India's institutional investment landscape.

items.title: Onion growers welcome agri market near Vadhvan port, urge govt to develop Nashik as export hub
items.summary: Maharashtra's onion growers welcome a new agricultural market near Vadhvan port. They propose developing Nashik as a national onion export hub for global trade. This initiative aims to boost farmer incomes and India's position in the onion market. The growers seek direct farmer benefits and improved export infrastructure. A stable export policy and processing incentives are also requested.

items.title: Xtranet Technologies announces IPO dates for launch next week. Check details
items.summary: Xtranet Technologies will launch its Rs 166.8 crore IPO on July 23 at a price band of Rs 120-127 per share. The issue, comprising a fresh issue of 1.31 crore shares, will fund debt repayment, capital expenditure and working capital, with listing scheduled on July 30.

items.title: Buy or sell: Sumeet Bagadia recommends three stocks to buy on Monday - 20 July 2026
items.summary: Sumeet Bagadia of Choice Broking, highlighted that from a technical perspective, the index is now approaching the crucial resistance zone near 200-Day EMA, where a decisive breakout could trigger further upside momentum.

items.title: Longer-dated yields lower after data, on track for weekly decline
items.summary: Longer-dated U.S. Treasury yields declined on Friday after recent economic data. Markets largely priced out a Federal Reserve rate hike for this month. Import prices increased unexpectedly, while consumer and producer prices showed moderation. Geopolitical worries and hawkish Fed comments added to market uncertainty. Bond yields moved lower, suggesting a flight to safety amid rising oil prices.

items.title: Czech government cuts bond issuance plan after strong demand for retail bonds
items.summary: Czech Finance Ministry will reduce planned bond issuance by 130 billion crowns. This decision follows strong demand for retail bonds offered recently. Overall gross borrowing needs for 2026 remain largely unchanged. Two more retail bond offerings are scheduled for later this year. This comes after 74 billion crowns sold in the first round.

items.title: Oil settles up on renewed US-Iran hostilities and threat of Red Sea closure
items.summary: Oil prices climbed significantly on Friday, reaching their highest point in over a month. Escalating hostilities between the United States and Iran intensified attacks across the Gulf region. Shipping routes face potential closure, impacting global oil flows and prices. Saudi Arabia has redirected significant crude exports to avoid the Strait of Hormuz. Ukraine also reported striking a Russian oil refinery in its conflict.

items.title: Gold set for biggest weekly drop since early June on inflation, rate-hike worries
items.summary: Gold prices saw an increase on Friday, driven by escalating U.S.-Iran tensions. These geopolitical events fueled energy prices and inflation concerns, impacting market sentiment. Higher inflation fears reinforced expectations of potential U.S. interest rate hikes by the Federal Reserve. While gold is a hedge against inflation, higher rates typically pressure the non-yielding metal. Other precious metals like silver, platinum, and palladium also experienced weekly losses.

items.title: Tech selloff weighs on European shares ahead of ECB meeting next week
items.summary: European stocks edged lower Friday, influenced by a global tech sell-off and Middle East tensions. Investors await the European Central Bank meeting and corporate earnings reports next week. Rising oil prices and subdued reactions to strong results complicated the market backdrop. Tech stocks experienced a significant decline this week, impacting major AI beneficiaries. Utilities and defense sectors saw gains, while luxury brands faced headwinds from reduced tourist spending.

items.title: IPO calendar: 5 IPOs opening for subscription to keep investors busy; SBI Funds among 4 listings scheduled
items.summary: The IPO market will remain active next week with five public issues opening for subscription, including mainboard offerings from Xtranet Technologies and Cube Highways Trust InvIT. Investors will also watch four listings, led by SBI Funds Management, as strong demand and healthy grey market premiums keep primary market sentiment buoyant.

items.title: JSW Steel’s Q1 profit soars 2x YoY on robust topline growth
items.summary: JSW Steel's profit more than doubled year-on-year to 4,696 crore in the June quarter. Robust revenue growth and higher volumes aided the company's strong financial performance. Consolidated revenue from operations rose around ten percent on year to 47,364 crore. Total expenses grew less than four percent on year, which helped profitability. The company's net debt also saw a significant reduction during the period.

items.title: ITC hotels announces acquisition of 130 key operating hotel in Ahmedabad’s commercial hub
items.summary: The addition of this hotel would further strengthen the company’s owned asset portfolio in Ahmedabad by straddling across all the branded hospitality market segments and price points, the company said.

items.title: Wall Street's chip index enters bear market! Is the AI bubble finally going bust?
items.summary: Wall Street's semiconductor index slipped into bear market territory after tumbling more than 20% from its June peak, raising fresh questions over the sustainability of the AI-driven rally. A new AI model from China's Moonshot and concerns over heavy AI spending triggered a broad selloff across global technology stocks.

items.title: BSE, Sona BLW among 10 midcap stocks that rose up to 53% in 2026. Do you own any?
items.summary: Midcap stocks have staged a strong comeback in 2026, with Oracle Financial Services, Solar Industries and BSE delivering gains of up to 53% as market sentiment and earnings outlook improved.

items.title: Fairfax picks up half of 3-year government bonds at auction
items.summary: Fairfax Financial Holdings likely purchased government bonds as a prelude to acquiring IDBI Bank.  This marks the company's second significant bond purchase in recent months.  The Canadian firm is reportedly preparing to invest capital for a potential IDBI Bank stake.  Fairfax aims to secure a major stake in the government-owned lender.  The IDBI Bank privatisation process has been ongoing since October 2022.

items.title: NIfty IT logs best weekly gains since Oct 2025
items.summary: Indian IT stocks experienced a significant rebound this week.  The Nifty IT index saw its best weekly gain since October 2025.  This rally followed a sell-off in Asian AI and semiconductor stocks.  First-quarter earnings from major IT firms showed minimal AI disruption.  Investor confidence improved, leading to some short-covering in the sector.

items.title: Reliance Retail's Q1 profit drops 14% YoY but revenue rises 8%
items.summary: Reliance Retail Ventures reported a fourteen percent profit decline last quarter. Revenue from operations grew eight point two percent year-on-year. Investments in e-commerce and digital infrastructure pressured operating margins. The company aims to double operating Ebitda over three years. Reliance Retail expanded its store network to over twenty thousand locations.

items.title: Indian pharma sector set for steady growth in FY27 on domestic demand: Report
items.summary: 360 ONE Capital expects the sector to deliver 10% year-on-year revenue growth in Q1 FY27

items.title: 200% Dividend Per Share: Manufacturing firm to give free cash reward; record date this week
items.summary: 200% Dividend Per Share By Forgings Manufacturer: Happy Forgings Ltd's record date for its final dividend is falling next week. The forgings manufacturing firm has fixed July 20, 2026, as the record date to determine shareholders eligible to receive its final dividend. Check details:

items.title: Stocks to buy under  ₹200: Mehul Kothari of Anand Rathi recommends three shares to buy or sell
items.summary: Mehul Kothari from Anand Rathi suggests a cautious approach for traders, recommending a decisive close above 24,400 for a bullish outlook. For stocks under  ₹200, he recommends Suzlon Energy, IDBI, and Zee Entertainment with specified buy prices and targets.

items.title: D-Street bucks Asian markets' meltdown
items.summary: The NSE’s Nifty 50 rose 261.55 points, or 1.1%, to close at 24,334.3, while the BSE Sensex gained 964.58 points, or 1.25%, to end at 78,151.45.

items.title: Investors take to gold dip as Mideast war rages on
items.summary: Gold prices are experiencing their steepest weekly decline in six weeks. This drop has encouraged investors to return to the yellow metal. Consumers are also returning to jewelry stores after recent price reductions. Investors are showing caution towards silver, reducing their exposure. Gold remains a preferred core portfolio asset for many investors.

items.title: RIL's Q1 operational revenue hits milestone with 25% YoY jump
items.summary: Reliance Industries saw a twenty-five percent profit drop in the June quarter.  Revenue from operations increased twenty-five percent, crossing three lakh crore rupees.  Reliance Retail's net profit declined fourteen percent while revenue grew eight percent.  The oil-to-chemicals segment revenue rose over thirty percent amid market volatility.  Jio Platforms reported its first profit decline due to 5G asset capitalization.

items.title: Small rivers need distinct conservation strategy, says NMCG draft framework
items.summary: The framework prioritises natural connectivity, catchment restoration, local water storage and nature-based solutions tailored to local ecosystems

items.title: Volvo seals one of Q2's biggest office deals with 600,000 sq ft Bengaluru lease
items.summary: India's office market recorded its strongest-ever quarter in Q2 2026, with gross leasing reaching a record 24.6 million sq ft, according to CBRE. Volvo Group signed one of the quarter's largest deals, leasing 600,000 sq ft in Bengaluru, as GCCs, flexible workspaces and technology firms continued to drive demand despite geopolitical uncertainty.

items.title: Wall Street Week Ahead: Alphabet, Intel results in focus for AI trade as US earnings rev up
items.summary: Corporate earnings season is gaining momentum with key tech updates expected soon. Investors anticipate strong profit growth, which has supported stock market enthusiasm. Alphabet's AI spending plans will be closely watched for market-wide impacts. Semiconductor stocks face volatility as expectations for the sector remain high. Geopolitical tensions and inflation fears add to market uncertainty ahead of Fed meetings.

items.title: Axis Bank Q1 preview: Profit, NII to stay healthy; NIM likely to shrink due to deposit repricing, competitive funding
items.summary: As Axis Bank gears up to announce its Q1 results, analysts predict significant growth in net interest income and net profit. However, a potential decline in net interest margin due to competitive pressures and deposit repricing raises concerns.

items.title: ICICI Bank Q1 results preview: Net profit seen up 3.1% YoY to  ₹13,164 crore; NIMs, asset quality may remain stable
items.summary: ICICI Bank Q1 results preview: ICICI Bank’s loan growth in the June quarter is expected to be healthy at 18.5% YoY, led by gold loans, corporate loans, personal loans and mortgages. Deposits are seen rising 15.2% YoY.

items.title: HDFC Bank Q1 results preview: Net profit likely to rise 6% YoY on strong loan growth, NIMs to remain flat
items.summary: HDFC Bank Q1 results preview: HDFC Bank Q1 results are expected to be mixed, with NIM likely to remain largely stable sequentially, while asset quality is expected to remain steady. Analysts anticipate healthy double-digit loan growth, supported by strong business momentum.

items.title: Q1 results 2026: HDFC Bank to ICICI Bank, Axis Bank among over 20 companies to declare Q1 earnings today; full list here
items.summary: On July 18, over 20 companies, primarily banks, will announce their Q1 FY27 earnings, providing crucial insights into India's banking sector. Key indicators include profit growth and asset quality, with strong focus on management commentary amid rising geopolitical tensions.

items.title: Preparing India’s sugar mills for the upcoming crushing season
items.summary: The approach now involves managing choices between sugar, ethanol, and power, and maximising value from every tonne of cane

items.title: Why global agricultural macroeconomic frameworks fail without ‘farmer health capital’
items.summary: Global institutions must move from theory to execution through three targeted interventions

items.title: Shyam Metalics commissions aluminium foil facility at Sambalpur plant in Odisha
items.summary: Shyam Metalics and Energy has commissioned a new aluminium foil manufacturing facility. This facility in Sambalpur, Odisha, has an annual production capacity of eighteen thousand tonnes. The company invested approximately eight hundred crore rupees in downstream aluminium facilities. Their aluminium Flat Rolled Products facility is nearing completion and will launch by September. This expansion strengthens their value-added aluminium products portfolio and economic growth.

items.title: US firms sign $60 billion Iraq deals to develop oil routes beyond Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: Iraq is positioning itself as a long-term investment hub while diversifying oil export routes through Syria and Turkey

items.title: Why Netflix’s AI push isn’t reviving the stock
items.summary: The streamer says that gen-AI workflows now have been used in about 300 of the company’s titles.

items.title: These three large-cap stocks look cheap. Here's why investors should look closer
items.summary: These three large-cap stocks trade at relatively low valuations versus peers, but investors should look beyond cheap P/E multiples before making a decision.

items.title: FAA restores Boeing’s authority to certify 737 Max and 787 aircraft
items.summary: The decision follows months of joint FAA-Boeing safety reviews.

items.title: RCPL Q1 FY27: Campa, Independence drive revenue past ₹8,600 crore; Australia launch in July
items.summary: RCPL is building a national FMCG platform as it targets ₹1 lakh crore in annual revenue by FY30

items.title: Reliance Industries Q1 FY27: Oil-to-Chemicals EBITDA rises 17%; solar and battery manufacturing plans advance
items.summary: Reliance used its integrated energy business to weather supply shocks while advancing solar and battery manufacturing

items.title: Reliance Retail targets higher margins from digital commerce push over two years
items.summary: Reliance Retail will prioritise repeat customers and fulfilment efficiency in its digital expansion

items.title: Excuse me while I speak to my knuckles
items.summary: Absurd wearable gadgets are bringing AI closer— but driving people apart.

items.title: Bish Agrawal: The man who created India's executive search industry
items.summary: Long before executive search became a billion-dollar business, Bish Agrawal bet on an industry India didn't know it needed—and built the country's first professional leadership hiring firm.

items.title: Mint Explainer | Why the UPI merchant fee debate is back in focus
items.summary: UPI processed 22.72 billion transactions worth  ₹28.92 trillion in June, averaging 757 million transactions per day. This scale has revived questions about who pays to keep the system running.

items.title: Bank Holiday Next Week (July 20–26): SBI, HDFC, ICICI & other banks closed for 3 days; check full list
items.summary: Bank Holiday Next Week: As the fourth week of July 2026 approaches, Indian bank customers should keep track of the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) holiday calendar before planning branch visits in next week. From July 20 to July 26, banks will remain closed for three days in different parts of India.

items.title: The week in charts: Inflation heat, export shift, tracking services production
items.summary: In this weekly Plain Facts compilation, we present to you data-based insights, with easy-to-read charts, to help you delve deeper into the stories reported by Mint in the week gone by.

items.title: It's a revamp! RBI steps ahead on plan for plastic rupee notes
items.summary: India's currency printing authority is now on the lookout for international suppliers of polymer banknote material, signaling a significant change from nearly a century of using paper notes. Bidders must adhere to strict security protocols and provide evidence of past collaborations with central banks. The Reserve Bank of India is considering polymer notes due to their durability, coinciding with a recent drop in currency printing costs.

items.title: Beaten down IT majors catch fancy of prop traders
items.summary: Selling cash shares borrowed from HNI clients , buying futures on IT stocks yields high single-digit gains for traders

items.title: FIFA final gives weekend spending a perfect assist
items.summary: Indian restaurants and pubs anticipate a significant spending surge this weekend. Advance bookings for venues have more than doubled, prompting extended operating hours. Television manufacturers report a substantial increase in large-screen TV demand. Brands are offering special promotions and meal combos to capitalize on the event. Businesses expect a considerable boost in sales during the final match.

items.title: US stocks today: Nasdaq, S&P fall over 1%, end lower for week as chip selloff broadens
items.summary: Wall Street declined as AI-driven chip stocks extended losses, triggering broader risk-off sentiment. Semiconductor shares saw sharp weekly declines, while energy stocks gained on rising oil prices amid Middle East tensions. Despite strong early earnings, including bank results, weak forecasts from companies like Netflix weighed on investor confidence.

items.title: IDBI Bank ups FCNR ante with 16% on offer
items.summary: The scheme allows customers to borrow up to 12 times their deposit amount and offers indicative effective annual returns of up to 16.2%, depending on the investment size and tenure, according to offer documents accessed by ET. Under the FCNR(B) Special Opportunity Deposit with Structured Loan Facility, the bank is offering dollar deposit rates of up to 6.60% for deposits above $5 million with maturities of three to five years.

items.title: Macros strong, but weak rains, war can hit growth: RBI Guv Sanjay Malhotra
items.summary: The Indian economy expanded 7.7% in the last financial year, and the RBI has projected GDP growth of 6.6% for the current fiscal year despite global uncertainties, Sanjay Malhotra said. The governor also flagged inflation risks, noting that the RBI had raised its inflation forecast for FY27 to 5.1% from 4.6% earlier.

items.title: PM Modi flags off India's first hydrogen-powered train
items.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi flagged off India's first hydrogen-powered train in Haryana, calling it the world's most powerful hydrogen train with 3,200 horsepower. Operating between Jind and Sonipat, the train marks India's entry into hydrogen rail technology, with Modi signalling a wider rollout after further research to improve efficiency and reduce costs.

items.title: RIL charts multi-year plan to offset decline in natural gas output
items.summary: Reliance Industries plans a multi-year campaign to boost natural gas output.  This effort aims to offset declining production from existing fields.  Elevated gas prices are expected due to ongoing West Asia conflict.  The company anticipates better price realization for its KG-D6 gas fields.  Continued investment in coal-bed methane operations will also proceed.

items.title: 'Air India to stay on expansion course despite headwinds,' says CCO Nipun Aggarwal
items.summary: Air India plans ambitious expansion, doubling capacity and adding new global destinations.  The airline aims to connect every major city worldwide with India.  This expansion continues despite significant financial losses and operational disruptions.  Pakistani airspace closure and rising fuel costs impact flight routes and expenses.  Employees urge a swift appointment of a new chief executive officer.

items.title: Subscription, digital Ad sales boost JioStar show
items.summary: JioStar's Ebitda from operations increased thirty-one percent year-on-year. Higher subscription revenue and digital entertainment advertising fueled this growth. JioHotstar achieved its highest-ever average monthly active users. IPL 2026 became the most-watched season in the tournament's history. The micro-content platform Tadka crossed one hundred million active users.

items.title: Reliance Retail trades near-term gains for long-term digital access
items.summary: Reliance Retail reported a net profit decline of fourteen percent year-on-year. Revenue from operations grew eight point two percent, with FMCG business growth strong. Investments in digital commerce and infrastructure impacted operating margins significantly. The company aims to double operating Ebitda over three years through growth. Reliance Retail expanded its store network to over twenty thousand locations.

items.title: Iron ore joins core sector as government revamps index
items.summary: Iron ore has been added to the core sector industries list, expanding it to nine. The government will release a revised Index of Core Industries series on July 20. This new series will feature a base year of 2022-23, replacing the older 2011-12 base. Raw coal remains, but coal middlings and washed coal are excluded to prevent double counting.

items.title: Indian film-makers fear to tread many paths these days
items.summary: Creators face increasing self-censorship in India's entertainment industry. Sensitive topics like Kashmir and caste are now avoided at ideation stages. Fear of social media backlash and regulatory scrutiny limits creative expression. Writers now make strategic decisions rather than purely creative ones. This trend is significantly shrinking the universe of available storytelling.

items.title: EPFO launches VISHWAS 2026 to settle employer penalty disputes
items.summary: The EPFO has launched VISHWAS 2026, a six-month, one-time dispute resolution scheme to help employers settle pending damages and penalty cases under provident fund laws. The initiative offers substantially reduced penalty rates for eligible defaults before June 14, 2024, through a fully digital, transparent and time-bound process.

items.title: Vande Bharat sleeper trains' rollout delayed as designs await approval
items.summary: The rollout of 120 Vande Bharat sleeper trains by Indo-Russian joint venture Kinet Railway Solutions has been delayed as final train designs are yet to be approved. With the prototype now expected only by the end of 2026, Indian Railways has turned to its own manufacturing units to accelerate sleeper coach production.

items.title: Centre rolls out Rs 5,175 crore mine reform incentive scheme
items.summary: The Centre has issued guidelines for states to claim ₹5,175 crore in incentives under the SASCI 2026-27 scheme by accelerating mine operationalisation, boosting mineral production and implementing mining reforms. States must operationalise 79 auctioned mineral blocks by December 31 to qualify, with incentives linked to clearances and governance performance.

items.title: Banks seek RBI clarity on collateral-free loans up to Rs 20 lakh for small businesses
items.summary: Banks face challenges extending collateral-free loans up to twenty lakh rupees.  Some accounts are not covered by the government guarantee scheme for small firms.  Many borrowers are unwilling to pay additional guarantee fees for these loans.  Lenders plan to approach the government and RBI regarding these issues.  This situation impacts credit access for micro and small enterprises.

items.title: A trade deal that tests India’s competitive confidence
items.summary: Trade agreements reward nations ready to compete

items.title: Egg prices soar in India as maize, soybean costs push up poultry expenses
items.summary: In a shocking turn of events, egg prices in India have soared to historic highs, primarily due to surging poultry feed costs. Increases in maize and soybean meal prices are heavily influencing production expenses, alongside ongoing concerns related to the West Asia conflict and local crop yields. Furthermore, the intense summer heat has negatively impacted broiler chicken production, leading to a surge in meat prices.

items.title: Sugar prices hit record highs amid supply concerns in India
items.summary: Record high sugar prices in India have emerged due to pressing supply issues, though industry leaders assure that domestic stocks are adequate for consumer needs. This rise in prices has mainly transpired over the last month, influenced by low inventories in Maharashtra and Karnataka. Optimistically, the upcoming season is expected to see a notable boost in production.

items.title: Government hikes duty drawback for jewellery exports
items.summary: The duty drawback for gold jewellery and parts has been increased to ₹1,851.99 per gram from ₹773.17 per gram, while the rate for silver jewellery and articles made of silver has been raised to ₹29,501.09 per kg from ₹14,990.66 per kg, according to a notification issued by the Department of Revenue on Friday.

items.title: Government proposes mandatory energy ratings for large buildings above 20,000 sq metres
items.summary: Mandatory energy performance ratings are set to be introduced for sizable commercial buildings over 20,000 square metres in India. This pivotal move aims to elevate energy efficiency within the burgeoning building industry, allowing the government to standardize evaluations and promote superior building designs. Consequently, consumers will have the opportunity to assess and compare energy efficiency ratings across various properties.

items.title: Bharat Tex 2026 attracts $2.8 billion business enquiries, ₹14,300 crore textile investments
items.summary: At Bharat Tex 2026, business inquiries reached $2.8 billion, complemented by investments of ₹14,300 crore. Andhra Pradesh and Karnataka have taken a leading role in proposing substantial textile investments nationwide. The recent India-UK FTA is poised to enhance India's textile and apparel export growth significantly. As the country broadens its free trade agreement scope, expectations are set for exports to hit ₹9 lakh crore by 2030.

items.title: 'Closely following': India on US bill seeking 100% tariffs on India, others
items.summary: India on Friday said it is closely monitoring a proposed US bill that seeks to impose 100% tariffs on countries, including India, buying Russian crude oil. The External Affairs Ministry said India's energy imports are guided by its sourcing strategy. The bipartisan US bill aims to curb Russia's oil revenue funding the Ukraine war.

items.title: Saudi Arabia begins regaining share in India's crude imports
items.summary: Saudi Arabia is expected to supply 464,000 barrels per day (bpd) to India in July, based on arrivals in the first half of the month and tanker-tracking data. Between July 2025 and April 2026, the kingdom supplied an average of 700,000 bpd, accounting for about 14.5% of India's crude imports. After its share slipped to an average 7% in May-June, Saudi Arabia is set to recover to about 10% in July.

items.title: Reliance earnings drop 22% in Q1 on one time stake sale; Revenue up 25%
items.summary: Strong O2C recovery and rising digital services at Jio offset Retail margin pressure, helping Reliance beat EBITDA expectations

items.title: A hit movie and a cricket festival power profits at JioStar
items.summary: JioStar reported a net profit increase of 14.5% to Rs. 665 crore driven by subscriptions and digital ads, supported by IPL engagement. Revenue rose 14% to Rs. 10,946 crore, and monthly users for JioHotstar grew 15% to 530 million, highlighting robust digital growth amidst challenges.

items.title: RIL Q1 PAT falls 22% to ₹20,946 crore, revenue up 25%
items.summary: “The O2C business delivered strong performance during the quarter, supported by all-time high middle distillate cracks despite a challenging global energy market backdrop with disrupted supply chains,” Mukesh Ambani said

items.title: India-EU Investment Protection Pact, GI agreements work in progress: Piyush Goyal
items.summary: India and the European Union are progressing on investment protection and GI agreements. These pacts will create significant business opportunities for both sides. India is now connected to the entire European market through recent trade agreements. Estonia offers opportunities in education, defense, and skill development sectors. India has implemented reforms to attract long-term foreign direct investment.

items.title: Reliance Retail bets on JioMart scale to drive margins, growth
items.summary: Reliance Retail plans disciplined expansion of JioMart’s dark-store network, betting on higher order density and private labels to improve margins as competition in quick commerce intensifies.

items.title: DCM Shriram to acquire 26% stake in Serentica Renewables for ₹105 crore
items.summary: The investment will support a 58 MW hybrid renewable energy project to power DCM Shriram’s energy-intensive operations in Bharuch, Gujarat.

items.title: Reliance tops estimates as petrochemicals business stands firm in a turbulent first quarter
items.summary: Reliance Industries' earnings beat came despite expectations that geopolitical tensions and volatile energy markets would weigh on Reliance’s flagship oil-to-chemicals (O2C) business, which contributes more than half of the company’s consolidated revenue.

items.title: JSW Steel Q1 profit, revenue beat estimates on higher prices, strong demand
items.summary: The Sajjan Jindal-led company reported a consolidated net profit of  ₹4,651 crore for the first quarter of FY27, up from  ₹2,184 crore a year earlier, according to an exchange filing on Friday. The profit exceeded the  ₹3,199-crore average estimate of seven analysts polled by Bloomberg.

items.title: Government making all efforts for early release of AI-171 crash report: Naidu
items.summary: The government is working to expedite the Air India flight AI-171 crash probe report.  Minister K Rammohan Naidu stated the investigation is ongoing and resources are provided.  He emphasized that rushing the process could compromise the truth and justice for victims.  The Aircraft Accident Investigation Bureau expects the draft report by October.  India's civil aviation sector is experiencing significant growth and momentum.

items.title: RBI imposes penalties on Muthoot Finance, 5 others for compliance violations
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has imposed penalties on six financial companies. Muthoot Finance received a Rs 5.80 lakh fine for risk management failures. Avail Financial Services was fined for director conflicts and exposure breaches. PAN Emami Cosmed faced penalties for exceeding credit exposure limits. Satya MicroCapital was penalized for incorrect NPA classification after restructuring.

items.title: Bharat Tex generates $2 billion worth enquiries
items.summary: Bharat Tex catalyses around ₹14,300 crore of textile investment commitments with more than 30 MoUs signed

items.title: Reliance Retail Q1 net profit down 14%; FMCG revenue doubles
items.summary: Reliance Retail said it will focus on Jiomart as a key lever for growth in the next four quarters

items.title: Reliance Retail Q1 revenue rises, but profit falls as e-commerce weighs
items.summary: Reliance Retail's Q1 revenue rose 8.2% despite the FMCG demerger, while net profit fell 14.2% as e-commerce investments pressured margins. Reliance Consumer more than doubled revenue, driven by Campa and Independence, with rapid growth across grocery, electronics and digital commerce.

items.title: West Asia crisis, uncertain monsoon major risks for growth: RBI Governor
14,000 feet up: Deep well drilling completed for India's first geothermal plant in Puga Valley
items.summary: In a groundbreaking move, India has initiated its first-ever geothermal wells in Ladakh's Puga Valley, delving deep at 1,000 meters. Despite these efforts, progress has been limited; government support and incentives could provide a boost to the sector
